REPLY IN SUPPORT OF GENERAL MOTORS LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES FROM PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 210060619) FILED BY DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE FEB-05-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES FROM PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. AS ANALYZED ABOVE, THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT SETS FORTH A VIABLE CLAIM FOR FRAUDULENT CONCEALMENT, AND PLAINTIFF MAY RECOVER PUNITIVE DAMAGES BASED ON THIS CLAIM. (CIV. CODE 3294). ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED (302/RBU)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-04-2025 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-13-2024 Set For Trial On Apr-01-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-05-2024 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. SIYUN YANG, Esq. of Quill& Arrow, LLP (Tel: 310-933-4271) for Plaintiffs.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Matter not argued/contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant General Motors LLC's ("GM") demurrer to plaintiff's cause of action for concealment in the first amended complaint ("FAC") is overruled.

A claim for fraudulent concealment requires that: (1) the defendant concealed or suppressed a material fact; (2) the defendant was under a duty to disclose the fact to the plaintiff; (3) the defendant intentionally concealed or suppressed the fact with intent to defraud the plaintiff; (4) the plaintiff was unaware of the fact and would have acted otherwise if he had known; and (5) as a result, the plaintiff sustained damages. (Boschma v. Home Loan Center, Inc. (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 230, 248.) Fraud generally must be plead with specificity. (Linear Technology Corp. v. Applied Materials, Inc. (2007) 152 Cal.App.4th 115, 132. But less specificity may be required where the facts lie more in the knowledge of the opposite party (as in this concealment case). (Alfaro v. Community Housing Improvement System & Planning Assn., Inc. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1356, 1384.)

There are "four circumstances in which nondisclosure or concealment may constitute actionable fraud: (1) when the defendant is in a fiduciary relationship with the plaintiff; (2) when the defendant had exclusive knowledge of material facts not known to the plaintiff; (3) when the defendant actively conceals a material fact from the plaintiff; and (4) when the defendant makes partial representations but also suppresses some material facts. [Citation.]" (Heliotis v. Schuman (1986) 181 Cal.App.3d 646, 651.) "Active concealment or suppression of facts by a nonfiduciary 'is the equivalent of a false representation, i.e., actual fraud.' [Citation.]" (Vega v. Jones, Day, Reavis & Pogue (2004) 121 Cal.App.4th 282, 291.)

Plaintiff alleged that since 2014, GM was aware that vehicles equipped with the same or similar transmission displayed the same defects due to testing, consumer complaints, and other internal sources (FAC pars. 8, 44-47); GM actively concealed the defects when its authorized repair facility represented to plaintiff that the transmission could be fixed (FAC pars. 128-129, 140). Plaintiff also alleges exclusive knowledge. (FAC, par. 134.) The specific identity of the GM personnel involved are more known to GM than to plaintiff. Plaintiff's allegations suffice at the pleading stage to show active concealment. (Daugherty v. Am. Honda Motor Co. (2006) 144 Cal.App.4th 824, 836 [manufacturer's duty to disclose based on safety concerns]; Falk v. General Motors Corp. (N.D. Cal.2007) 496 F.Supp.2d 1088 [plaintiff pleaded concealment claim based on GM's failure to disclose defects re speedometers]; Dhital v. Nissan North America, Inc. (2022) 84 Cal.App.5th 828, 844[vehicle buyer pleaded viable concealment claim against manufacturer for concealing defects (review granted-cited for persuasive value only (CRC 8.1115(e)(1))].)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported (302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-05-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. SIYUN YANG, Esq. of Quill & Arrow, LLP (Tel: 310-933-4271) for Plaintiffs.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT.

Matter not argued/contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant General Motors LLC's motion to strike the claim for punitive damages from plaintiff's first amended complaint is denied. As analyzed above, the first amended complaint sets forth a viable claim for fraudulent concealment, and plaintiff may recover punitive damages based on this claim. (Civ. Code 3294.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported (302/RBU)
